content.verdict: Hotel B is Lima's most stylish boutique stay — a 1914 Belle Époque mansion in the heart of the Barranco art district, lovingly restored as a Relais & Châteaux property with just 17 rooms and an active contemporary art gallery on the ground floor. For honeymooners who treat Lima as more than a flight-connection city, this is the most romantic urban opening or closing chapter for a Peru itinerary. The mansion's original Tiffany stained glass, marble floors, and high-ceilinged salons still set the tone, but the artwork on the walls rotates with the gallery's program — guests sleep beside Latin American contemporary pieces that change every few months. The rooftop terrace is one of Barranco's best sundown spots, the afternoon tea in the salon is a small ritual that has become an institution, and Mario Testino's MATE photography museum is a five-minute walk away. Lima's celebrated culinary scene — Maido, Central, Kjolle, Astrid y Gastón — is at most a short drive, and the hotel's concierge handles those near-impossible reservations. Boutique, design-forward, and quietly intellectual, Hotel B is for couples who want their honeymoon to start or end with culture, not poolside.
content.best_room: Book a Deluxe Room with balcony — these front-facing rooms have the original mansion's high ceilings, polished wood floors, and balconies opening onto the Barranco street life. The B Suite is the showstopper with a separate sitting room and a freestanding tub, ideal for honeymoon nights bookending a long Peru itinerary. Avoid interior-facing standard rooms if natural light matters to you.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Lima arrival, Hotel B check-in
itinerary_7_nights.description: Land in Lima, taxi to Barranco. Welcome pisco sour in the salon, late lunch on the rooftop, walk to MATE museum and the Bridge of Sighs. Dinner at Isolina or Central if you can secure a table.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Lima food and art
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning Larcomar cliffside walk in Miraflores, lunch at a cevichería. Afternoon at the Larco Museum and the Pre-Columbian gold collection. Evening cocktails on Hotel B's rooftop, dinner at Maido.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Fly to Cusco
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning flight to Cusco (3400m). Slow afternoon — coca tea, gentle stroll, no climbing. Overnight at Belmond Monasterio or Palacio Nazarenas.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Sacred Valley descent
itinerary_7_nights.description: Drive to the Sacred Valley via Pisac market and ruins. Lunch in Urubamba, afternoon at Ollantaytambo. Overnight at Sol y Luna or Tambo del Inka.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Vistadome to Machu Picchu
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning train from Ollantaytambo to Aguas Calientes. Full afternoon at the citadel with a private guide. Overnight at Sanctuary Lodge or Inkaterra.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Sunrise at Machu Picchu, return to Cusco
itinerary_7_nights.description: Pre-dawn return to the ruins. Train back through the valley, overnight in Cusco.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Fly Lima, final dinner, depart
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning flight to Lima, lunch at Astrid y Gastón, late afternoon at Barranco's galleries one last time, transfer to airport for international departure.

caveats: Lima's Pacific climate is grey and humid May-October — pack layers; Barranco can feel chilly at night even in summer.
Hotel B is a heritage mansion with creaky floors and small original elevators — part of the charm, but not silent or full-modern in feel.
Avoid the city center after dark on foot — Barranco itself is walkable and lively, but use taxis or Uber between districts.
At only 17 rooms it sells out fast, especially during food-week and art-fair calendar moments.

faqs.question: Is Hotel B good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Yes — for design-forward couples who care about food, art, and atmosphere, it's the most romantic Lima boutique. Less suited if you want resort amenities like a pool or full spa.

faqs.question: Where is it in Lima?
faqs.answer: In Barranco, the city's bohemian art district — a 15-minute taxi from Miraflores, 30-40 minutes from Lima airport, walking distance to MATE photography museum, the Bridge of Sighs, and a dozen great restaurants.

faqs.question: Will we get altitude sickness?
faqs.answer: No — Lima sits at sea level. Altitude becomes a factor only when you fly inland to Cusco. Many honeymooners spend their first 1-2 nights in Lima at sea level before going up.

faqs.question: Train or fly to Machu Picchu?
faqs.answer: From Lima you fly to Cusco, then train to Aguas Calientes. There is no direct rail or flight from Lima to Machu Picchu.

faqs.question: When is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: Lima's brightest months are December-March (summer); the rest of the year is grey but mild. The food and art calendars run year-round.

faqs.question: Can the concierge get us into top restaurants?
faqs.answer: Yes — Hotel B's relationships with Central, Maido, Kjolle, and Astrid y Gastón are part of the value. Request reservations at booking, not on arrival, as top tables are months out.
